label: Jurisdictions with Approved CRISPR Therapy (Casgevy)
source: FDA (Dec 2023), EMA (Feb 2024), MHRA (Nov 2023); plus Saudi Arabia, UAE, Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, Canada, Switzerland
deltaNote: Casgevy (exagamglogene autotemcel) is the world's first and only approved CRISPR medicine; pediatric sBLA (ages 5–11) expected in H1 2026

label: Active CRISPR Clinical Trials (Global)
source: ClinicalTrials.gov / IGI CRISPR Clinical Trials 2026 Update
deltaNote: Covering sickle cell, beta-thalassemia, cancer immunotherapy, vision, liver (ATTR, dyslipidemia), cardiovascular disorders

label: Casgevy List Price (One-Time Treatment)
contestNote: Payers and patient advocates dispute cost-effectiveness vs. chronic standard-of-care; access barriers remain severe in low-income countries

label: Patients with Access to Reimbursed Casgevy (US)
delta: From near-zero at launch to ~90% reimbursement coverage
deltaNote: Payer coverage now achieved for approximately 90% of eligible US patients; treatment ramp at authorized treatment centers accelerating in 2026

label: Global CRISPR Therapeutics Market (2024)
deltaNote: Includes therapeutics, tools, diagnostics, and agricultural CRISPR

label: CRISPR Research Publications (PubMed)
deltaNote: Exponential growth since 2013; field expanding to base editing, prime editing, CRISPRi/a

label: US Sickle Cell Patients (Potential Casgevy Eligibility)
source: NIH / CDC / American Society of Hematology
deltaNote: $2.2M price tag and complex treatment process create major access barriers; only a fraction will receive treatment

label: Years Since CRISPR-Cas9 Discovery
deltaNote: From first landmark Science paper to first approved human therapy: 11 years
